perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Premisys' Confidential Information to its employees with a bona fide need to know, but only to the extent reasonably necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Recipient agrees to instruct all such employees that they may not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Business Purpose and (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written consent of Premisys.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Premisys' Confidential Information to its employees. Recipient agrees to instruct all such employees that they may not disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written consent of Premisys.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'with a bona fide need to know, but only to the extent reasonably necessary to carry out the Business Purpose', the modified text suggests that Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any of its employees without the restriction of 'need to know' or being 'reasonably necessary', which creates an internal contradiction since the goal of the NDA is protecting information. It creates an in-text contradiction in location 3 where is states that third parties should not be informed of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited disclosure of Confidential Information to employees with a 'bona fide need to know' and 'only to the extent reasonably necessary'. The changed text removes these restrictions, allowing disclosure to any employee. The contradicted text states that the Recipient will not disclose to any third party the fact that confidential information has been disclosed. The Changed Text, by removing the 'need to know' restriction creates a contradiction because it broadens the scope of disclosure within the recipient's company which is relevant to outside parties knowing about the transaction

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.explanation: Removing '(except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder' creates a contradiction. Section 2 permits disclosure of confidential information to employees under certain conditions, but this change seemingly prohibits disclosing even the fact that confidential information was shared, which contradicts section 2 and creates an in-text contradiction. This in-text contradiction affects section 4 because now it is not clear when they should be notified to seek a protective order
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Information of Premisys, Recipient shall provide Premisys with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that Premisys may seek a protective order, confidential treatment or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If, in the absence of a protective order, confidential treatment or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by Premisys, Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required to make the disclosure and, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for Recipient, legally compelled to disclose Premisys' Confidential Information to any court, tribunal or agency or else stand liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such tribunal or agency only that portion of Premisys' Confidential Information which such counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed, provided that Recipient and its Representative exercise their best efforts to preserve the confidentiality of Premisys' Confidential Information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with Premisys to obtain an appropriate protective order or other assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded Premisys' Confidential Information by such tribunal or agency.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed disclosure of the fact that Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder, if permitted by the terms of the agreement. Section 2 of the contract permits disclosure of confidential information to employees under certain conditions. The changed text removes this exception, now seemingly prohibiting disclosing even the fact that confidential information was shared, which contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text requires recipient to notify Premisys if they are required to disclose any Confidential Information, so that Premisys may seek a protective order. However, the changed text prevents the recipient from disclosing even the fact that confidential information has been disclosed, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE").
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall comply with Premisys' instructions regarding Confidential Materials. Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE").
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific instructions for returning or destroying Confidential Materials creates an in-text contradiction. While a 'Termination Notice' still triggers an obligation to comply, the lack of specific directions now contradicts the implied expectation that there is a clear process, creating ambiguity and contradiction because there is no specifics instructions. It is not clear what the 'Compliance Certificate' should now entail and what measures should be taken, creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN/DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall comply with Premisys' instructions regarding Confidential Materials. Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event Recipient shall describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that upon receiving a Termination Notice, the Recipient must either return or destroy the Confidential Materials, or a combination of both. The changed text removes these specific options and replaces them with a general instruction to 'comply with Premisys' instructions regarding Confidential Materials.' This creates a contradiction because the 'COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE' referenced in both the changed and contradicted text implies a clear set of obligations that the Recipient must certify they have met. The original text provided that clear set of instructions, whereas the changed text doesn't, thus contradicting the expectation of a concrete process by which the Recipient can follow, and then, certify its compliance.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation arising from or relating to this Agreement, the party which is found to be the prevailing party by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final, non-appealable order shall receive from the non-prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses, including attorney fees, incurred by the prevailing party in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation arising from or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to legal fees.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specifics regarding 'court of competent jurisdiction in a final, non-appealable order' and 'reasonable legal fees and expenses', the modified text introduces significant ambiguity. It's unclear who determines the 'prevailing party', what constitutes 'legal fees', and if expenses are covered. This weakens the enforceability of the clause and is contradictory with what the agreement entails previously. This introduces contradiction in the interpretation of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INJUNCTIVE RELIEF; LEGAL FEES AND EXPENSES. Recipient acknowledges that any violation of the terms of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Premisys will have the right to obtain equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, as a remedy for any breach of the terms of this Agreement, as well as the right to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity for such a breach. In the event of litigation arising from or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to legal fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided specific criteria for legal fee recovery (court of competent jurisdiction, final non-appealable order, reasonable fees/expenses). The changed text removes these specifics, creating ambiguity about who determines the 'prevailing party' and what 'legal fees' entail. The contradicted text repeats the changed text ('In the event of litigation..., the prevailing party shall be entitled to legal fees'). Because the changed text lacks the clarity of the original and is repeated in the 'Contradicted Text' section, a contradiction exists. It's contradictory because the agreement as a whole is less enforceable due to the ambiguity, violating the intent of the initial agreement, and satisfies the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
